name: Neuromodulation & Stimulation
description: Vagus nerve stimulation (gammaCore), occipital nerve stimulation, deep brain stimulation. Device-based therapies.

all_trials.title: Anodal Transcranial Direct Stimulation (tDCS) for the Treatment of Chronic Cluster Headache
all_trials.what_tested: Transcranial direct current stimulation (tDCS) -- a gentle electrical brain stimulation applied to the forehead -- as a preventive treatment for chronic cluster headache.
all_trials.key_result: Completed with 32 participants but no results posted.
all_trials.patient_relevance: tDCS is non-invasive and could be done at home if effective -- but without published results we cannot know if it helps CH.

all_trials.title: nVNS for the Prevention and Treatment of Primary Headache
all_trials.what_tested: Non-invasive vagus nerve stimulation (nVNS) for acute treatment of primary headache in children and adolescents aged 7-20.
all_trials.key_result: Recruiting 15 participants, expected completion August 2026. This is primarily a migraine study in young patients, not specifically CH.
all_trials.patient_relevance: Mostly relevant to pediatric migraine, but could provide some data on vagus nerve stimulation in young headache patients generally.
all_trials.dose_tested: Non-invasive vagus nerve stimulation via ear-mounted device

all_trials.title: REsponse to Combined SONS and ONS in Chronic Cluster HeadachE
all_trials.what_tested: The PRIMUS system combining supraorbital nerve stimulation (SONS) and occipital nerve stimulation (ONS) for chronic cluster headache -- a first-in-human study.
all_trials.key_result: Active but no longer recruiting, with 5 participants. Expected completion April 2024.
all_trials.patient_relevance: A novel dual-site stimulation system that may work better than stimulating just one nerve -- very early stage with only 5 patients.
all_trials.dose_tested: PRIMUS combined SONS + ONS neurostimulation system

all_trials.title: A Randomized Multicentre Study for the Acute Relief of Episodic and Chronic Cluster Headache.
all_trials.what_tested: GammaCore (non-invasive vagus nerve stimulator) applied to the neck versus sham device for aborting acute cluster headache attacks.
all_trials.key_result: GammaCore showed 67 pain-free attacks out of 495 treated (13.5%) versus 46 out of 400 for sham (11.5%). The difference was modest and this trial had mixed results across episodic and chronic subtypes.
all_trials.patient_relevance: GammaCore showed a small benefit for acute CH attacks overall, but the advantage over sham was not dramatic -- it works better for episodic than chronic CH.
all_trials.dose_tested: GammaCore non-invasive vagus nerve stimulation device

all_trials.title: Non-invasive Neurostimulation of the Vagus Nerve for the Treatment of Cluster Headache
all_trials.what_tested: GammaCore (non-invasive vagus nerve stimulator) versus sham for acute treatment of cluster headache -- the pivotal regulatory trial.
all_trials.key_result: 16 of 44 GammaCore patients (36%) were responders versus 14 of 57 sham patients (25%). In the episodic CH subgroup, response rate was significantly higher with GammaCore (48% vs 6%). Chronic CH showed no significant difference.
all_trials.patient_relevance: GammaCore works for aborting episodic CH attacks but NOT for chronic CH -- it is now FDA-cleared based partly on this data, but benefit is limited to episodic patients.

all_trials.title: Refractory Chronic Cluster Headache: Exploring the Potential of Repetitive Transcranial Magnetic Stimulation
all_trials.what_tested: Repetitive transcranial magnetic stimulation (rTMS) versus sham stimulation for refractory chronic cluster headache -- 10 sessions over 2 weeks in a crossover design.
all_trials.key_result: Completed with 8 participants but no results posted. Very small pilot study.
all_trials.patient_relevance: TMS is non-invasive and already used for depression -- if it works for refractory CH, it could help the hardest-to-treat patients, but this was very small.
all_trials.dose_tested: rTMS for 10 minutes, 10 consecutive working days

all_trials.title: Evaluation of Efficacity and Safety of Deep Brain Stimulation (DBS) in Chronic and Treatment-Resistant Cluster Headache(CH)
all_trials.what_tested: Deep brain stimulation (DBS) with electrodes implanted in the posterior hypothalamus for treatment-resistant chronic cluster headache, in a crossover design with stimulation on vs off.
all_trials.key_result: Completed with 12 participants but no results posted. Efficacy defined as 50% or more reduction in weekly attacks.
all_trials.patient_relevance: DBS is an extreme last-resort treatment requiring brain surgery -- it is only for patients who have failed everything else, and results from this controlled study would be very informative.
all_trials.dose_tested: Deep brain stimulation of posterior-inferior hypothalamus

all_trials.title: A Research Study to Examine Cutaneous Allodynia and Cluster Headache
all_trials.what_tested: Whether cluster headache patients experience cutaneous allodynia (pain from normally non-painful touch) during and between attacks.
all_trials.key_result: 20 out of 41 CH patients showed allodynia (about 49%), demonstrating that nearly half of CH patients have abnormal pain sensitivity on the skin.
all_trials.patient_relevance: Nearly half of CH patients have skin sensitivity (allodynia) -- this suggests central sensitization occurs in CH, similar to migraine, which could affect treatment choices.

all_trials.title: DBS of the Third Ventricle for Cluster Headache and Obesity
all_trials.what_tested: Deep brain stimulation of the third ventricle area for both chronic cluster headache and obesity -- exploring shared hypothalamic pathways.
all_trials.key_result: Completed with 13 participants but no results posted. Study ran for over 10 years.
all_trials.patient_relevance: An unusual study targeting a different brain area than typical DBS for CH -- results could reveal connections between CH and metabolic regulation.
all_trials.dose_tested: Deep brain stimulation of the third ventricle

all_trials.title: Saccadometry in Primary Headache Syndromes
all_trials.what_tested: Whether eye-tracking measurements (saccadometry) show differences in brain function between migraine, cluster headache, and healthy people.
all_trials.key_result: Terminated after enrolling 42 participants. This was a diagnostic/mechanism study, not a treatment trial.
all_trials.patient_relevance: An attempt to find brain function differences in CH using eye-tracking -- terminated before completion, so no useful conclusions.

all_trials.title: Study of Hypothalamic Metabolism in Spontaneous Cluster Headache Attacks
all_trials.what_tested: Brain imaging (PET scan) study to precisely locate hypothalamic activation during spontaneous cluster headache attacks.
all_trials.key_result: Terminated after enrolling only 1 participant. Capturing a spontaneous attack during a brain scan is extremely difficult.
all_trials.patient_relevance: The hypothalamus is believed to be the "generator" of CH -- but imaging attacks in real-time proved nearly impossible in this study.
